name: Qoddi Application Hosting PaaS API
what_it_does: Container-based application hosting platform with REST API — enabling developers to deploy web applications in 30+ programming languages and frameworks from GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket, with automatic SSL, custom domains, environment variables, Cron jobs, and managed add-ons (Redis, PostgreSQL), at competitive pricing compared to Heroku.
use_cases: Deploying web applications via Qoddi API from GitHub/GitLab for CI/CD pipeline automation
Managing Qoddi app environment variables via API for configuration management
Provisioning Qoddi add-ons (Redis, PostgreSQL) via API for database setup
Querying Qoddi deployment logs and app status via API for monitoring
Scaling Qoddi app resources via API for automated capacity management
not_for: Enterprise workloads requiring SOC2, HIPAA, or compliance certifications
Applications requiring more than 8 GB RAM per instance
Teams needing Kubernetes-level control over infrastructure
best_when: A developer or small team needs a Heroku alternative with competitive pricing, 30+ language support, and simple deployment from Git — without managing infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need compliance certifications, large-scale enterprise deployment, or advanced networking.

auth.notes: API key from Qoddi dashboard. Bearer token auth. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhook for deployment events. GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket OAuth for repository connection. Environment variable management via API. No fine-grained access controls — single API key per account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 3 apps (512 MB RAM, shared CPU, sleep after 30 min inactivity)
pricing.notes: Israel-based company. Founded 2021. Bootstrapped. Heroku alternative focused on competitive pricing. 30+ programming language and framework support (Node.js, Python, Ruby, PHP, Go, Java, .NET, Elixir, etc.). Automatic SSL via Let's Encrypt. GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket integration. Add-ons: Redis, PostgreSQL. Custom domains. Cron jobs. US, EU, and APAC regions. Growing Heroku migration community. Low price point vs Heroku's post-free-tier pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for app and service management. Bearer token auth. Documentation is developing (newer platform). Webhook for deployment events. Git integration required for repo-based deploys. No compliance certifications. Rate limits not documented. Free tier apps sleep after 30 minutes — not suitable for always-on operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: App reads idempotent by name. Deployment triggers not idempotent. Environment variable sets idempotent by key.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier apps sleep after 30 minutes inactivity — add-ons needed for production
Young platform (2021) — API may change without notice
No compliance certifications — not for regulated industries
Rate limits undocumented — test before automation
Limited regions compared to major cloud providers

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. No compliance certifications. Bearer token auth. Environment variables encrypted at rest. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. No fine-grained access controls. Young company — security posture still maturing. Let's Encrypt SSL for custom domains.
